_instructions: One file per word. Each sense must have exactly ONE POS. If a word has multiple POS roles (e.g., Vi and N), create SEPARATE senses. Fields prefixed with _ are documentation -- strip them before import. TOCFL is the authoritative source for POS classification. Editorial corrections are tracked via alignment + source fields. ALL note fields (formula, usage_note, learner_traps) MUST use bilingual format: _en and _zh suffixes. Single-field versions are deprecated and will be flagged in review.
_variant_rule: Variant spellings (e.g., 佈告/布告, 佈告欄/布告欄) MAY cross-link as reciprocal synonym_related, but each variant entry must STAND ON ITS OWN: differentiated definitions, independent examples, its own relations beyond the variant link. Variants may NOT rely on each other to pad relation coverage. Variant inflation is a recurring audit flag.
_structure_guidance: For 3+ character words, use the dominant sub-structure or 'left-right' as the default. A 4-character idiom like 川流不息 typically reads as 'left-right' at the top level.

word._smart_id_format: Unicode codepoints separated by underscores. Single char: u + hex (u6d41 for 流). Multi-char: underscore-separated (u81a8_u8139 for 膨脹, u5ddd_u6d41_u4e0d_u606f for 川流不息).
word._alignment_note: Word-level alignment = worst of its senses. full = all POS match TOCFL. partial = we added editorial senses. disputed = we disagree with a TOCFL POS.

senses._sense_id_format: {smart_id}_{POS}_{sequence} -- for tracking; DB assigns its own IDs.
senses._pinyin_note: Use tone numbers after each syllable (liu2, peng2zhang4). One pronunciation per sense. Neutral tone = no number (de, ma).
_pos_options: V -- Action Verb, Transitive (買, 吃, 編輯 -- subject does something TO a direct object)
Vi -- Action Verb, Intransitive (哭, 坐, 辯論 -- activity with no direct object, no state change)
Vp -- Process Verb, Intransitive (死, 崩潰, 退休 -- subject undergoes state change, no object)
Vpt -- Process Verb, Transitive (完成, 打破, 發現 -- resultative complement + takes object)
Vpsep -- Separable Process Verb (生氣, 結婚 -- CLOSED category, 13 TOCFL words)
Vs -- Stative Verb (昂貴, 安詳, 健康 -- 很-test passes, no object, predicative)
Vsattr -- Stative Verb, Attributive ONLY (大型, 電動 -- 很-test FAILS, CLOSED category)
Vspred -- Stative Verb, Predicative ONLY (夠, 多, 少)
Vssep -- Separable Stative Verb (擔心, 傷心 -- CLOSED category, 7 TOCFL words)
Vst -- State-Transitive Verb (喜歡, 知道, 覺得 -- 在-test fails + takes object/complement)
Vaux -- Auxiliary Verb (能, 可以, 會 -- modal before main verb)
Vsep -- Separable Verb / V-O Compound (辦案, 唱歌, 走路 -- 了/modifier inserts between V and O)
Vcomp -- Resultative Complement (到, 住, 掉, 見, 開, 成, 好, 起 -- character acts as complement in V+C compounds)
CE -- Complement Expression (看得見, 等不及 -- complete V+得/不+Vcomp patterns)
N -- Noun
M -- Measure Word
Adv -- Adverb
Prep -- Preposition
Conj -- Conjunction
Ptc -- Particle
Det -- Determiner
Prn -- Pronoun
Num -- Number
Aux -- Auxiliary
Intj -- Interjection
IE -- Idiomatic Expression
Ph -- Phrase
_pos_critical_rules: V IS a valid sense-level tag -- the default transitive action verb. NOT a grouping label.
Each sense gets exactly ONE POS. If this word is also a Noun, create a separate sense.
Separable (Vsep) is ONLY for V-O compounds (動賓). V-V compounds with resultative complements are Vpt/Vp.
Object omission does NOT equal intransitive. A verb is transitive if it CAN take a direct object.
Vi = intransitive ACTION only. Intransitive change-of-state = Vp, NOT Vi.
Vpt/Vp = process verbs. If a resultative complement morpheme is present, it is Vpt (transitive) or Vp (intransitive). No further testing needed.

_pos_forced_test_order.step_1: Separable? Can you insert 了/過/modifier between V and O in modern speech? YES -> Vsep/Vpsep/Vssep
_pos_forced_test_order.step_2: Resultative complement? Common complements: 成到出上開掉斷破完入清光住見滿合回化取先後染立選敗去失. YES -> Vpt (takes object) or Vp (no object). STOP.
_pos_forced_test_order.step_3: 著-test: V+著+[object] natural? YES -> not process. Continue. NO -> strong evidence for Vp/Vpt.
_pos_forced_test_order.step_4: 在-test: 在+V natural? YES -> non-stative (Action/Process). NO -> Stative (go to step 6).
_pos_forced_test_order.step_5: Threshold crossing? Clear before->after state change? YES -> Vpt (object) or Vp (no object). NO -> V (object) or Vi (no object).
_pos_forced_test_order.step_6: Stative subcategories: Takes object -> Vst. 很-test passes, no object -> Vs. 很-test fails, attributive only -> Vsattr (CLOSED). Predicative only -> Vspred.

_pos_known_traps: 知道/喜歡/覺得 -> Vst (NOT Vpt -- 在-test fails, already IN the state)
明白 -> Vpt (白=complement, 沒明白 confirms unreached result)
離開/抵達 -> V (location verbs -- Chinese takes place as direct object)
安裝 -> V (durative activity, NOT Vpt -- no instantaneous snap)
閉/開 -> V (no complement in base verb; 閉上/開完 = separate Vpt entries)
唱歌/走路/說話 -> Vsep (V-O splits), NOT Vp
打電話 -> Vsep (NOT Vi or Vp)
senses._source_note: tocfl = from TOCFL official wordlist. editorial = added by our team (Louie/Guangliu/Huiming).
senses._alignment_note: full = matches TOCFL. partial = our editorial addition. disputed = we disagree with TOCFL's POS for this sense.
senses._alignment_workflow: When disputing a TOCFL POS: (1) mark the TOCFL sense as 'disputed', (2) create a new editorial sense with our corrected POS marked 'partial'. Both senses stay visible for learners.

definitions.en: to expand; to swell; to inflate

senses._definitions_note: Provide both English and Traditional Chinese. The definition must be specific to THIS sense and THIS POS only. Do NOT include separable notes like '(separable: X+Y)' -- the POS chip already communicates that.
senses._domains_REQUIRED: Flat array. Pick 1 to 4 domains from the frozen set below, ordered by relevance. Position 1 = most relevant (primary). Position 4 = least. Minimum 1. Maximum 4. No grouping at enrichment time — domains are a flat taxonomy; any navigational clustering in the admin UI is a separate presentational concern, not something you assign.
senses._domains_frozen_set: animals · appearance · art · body · business · cognition · communication · culture · daily-living · education · emotion · energy · environment · family · food · health · history · housing · identity · language · law · leisure · life · materials · media · medicine · money · movement · music · nature · number-quantity · objects · perception · personal · personality · philosophy · place · politics · properties · religion · safety · science · social-relations · society · sound · space · sports · technology · time · transportation · travel · values · weather · work (54 slugs — AUTHORITATIVE, matches DB)
senses._channel_note: Channel answers: 'Where is this word used?' Describes the spoken vs. written distribution spectrum.
senses._channel_rule: Do NOT use 'fluid' here. 'fluid' belongs in dimension (as dim-fluid). Channel is strictly about usage distribution: spoken vs. written.
senses._semantic_mode_CRITICAL: dim-fluid is a DIMENSION slug, NOT a semantic_mode slug. Don't confuse the two fields. Real catch: L4-batch-05 had semantic_mode='dim-fluid' on 不平 — caught by 惠明, R4a now blocks this automatically.
senses._dimension_note: Dimension answers: 'What kind of meaning is this?' Multi-select. Describes the pedagogical/semantic character of the word.

_dimension_definitions.internal: Inner life -- emotions, feelings, the unseen, essence. (懊惱, 惋惜, 憧憬)
_dimension_definitions.external: Outer world -- what can be seen, touched, measured. (彎曲, 威風, 旺盛)
_dimension_definitions.abstract: Abstract concepts -- ideas, systems, principles without physical form.
_dimension_definitions.dim-fluid: Meaning shifts across contexts -- semantic center moves depending on usage.
_dimension_definitions.temporal: Time-oriented -- duration, sequence, tense-like. (以前, 從此, 當時)
_dimension_definitions.aspectual: Aspect-marking -- how action unfolds over time. (了, 過, 著)
_dimension_definitions.grammatical: Purely grammatical function. (把, 比, 得)
_dimension_definitions.spatial: Space/location-oriented. (後, 前, 上, 下)
_dimension_definitions.pragmatic: Discourse/function -- structural or communicative. (為, 萬分, 為止)

senses._intensity_PURPOSE: Intensity grades the STRENGTH OF THE QUALITY the word denotes — how much of the thing is present in what the word describes. Not how loudly the speaker is expressing. The internal semantic content. 喜歡 denotes mild positive inclination; 愛 denotes a committed bond; 痴迷 denotes extreme fixation. The flower icons 🌸→🌺 map to how fully the quality has bloomed.
senses._intensity_STORAGE_VALUES: 0 = Not Applicable (explicit editorial decision — word has no strength gradient). 1-5 = Graded intensity. null = Not yet enriched/assessed (pending — UNFINISHED sense). Every submitted enriched sense MUST have 0 or 1-5. NEVER null. null is a VALIDATOR BLOCKER — submission will fail import.
senses._intensity_TWO_STAGE_DECISION: STAGE 1: Does this sense denote a quality with a strength gradient? If NO → intensity: 0 (Not Applicable), add _flags note naming why ('concrete noun' / 'function word' / 'measure word' / etc.), STOP. If YES → continue to Stage 2. STAGE 2 (only when Stage 1 = YES): Grade 1-5 on the scale below.
senses._intensity_canonical_family_positive_att: Vst gradient (positive valence holds across all 5): 1=心動/有好感 (first stirring, pre-like), 2=喜歡 (baseline like), 3=愛好 (established fondness), 4=愛 (committed love), 5=熱愛 (passionate love). Read top to bottom; feel the gradient.
senses._intensity_canonical_family_pure_intensi: Adv gradient (pure-intensity, neutral valence): 1=有點, 2=比較, 3=很, 4=非常, 5=極其. These words ARE intensity by nature — useful as cross-POS calibration anchors.
senses._intensity_cross_POS_calibration: Level 3 (moderate, present-but-not-extreme) anchors across POS: 愛好 (Vst), 很 (Adv), 好 (Vs), 熱情 (N), 喊 (V). If your 3 on one POS doesn't feel equivalent to 愛好/很/好/熱情/喊, recalibrate.
senses._intensity_valence_shift_pattern: Chinese vocabulary often exhibits valence shift at extreme intensity. Words that would be level 5 on a positive gradient often cross into negative-dominant or context-dependent connotation: 痴迷 (intensity 5 on attachment, but connotation negative-dominant — obsessive); 狂熱 (intensity 5 on enthusiasm, connotation context-dependent — fanatical). Intensity and connotation are INDEPENDENT FIELDS. Grade them separately. Note the pattern when grading level-5 words.
senses._intensity_not_applicable_examples: intensity: 0 is the CORRECT answer for: concrete nouns (桌子, 書, 水, 學生), function words (和, 的, 了, 嗎, 誰, 什麼), measure words (個, 條, 支), determiners/numerals (這, 那, 一, 第三), non-degree adverbs (已經, 正在 — temporal/aspectual, not degree), Vaux (modals), Vcomp (complement morphemes), and most Ptc/Conj/Prep/Det/M/Num/Prn senses. Rule of thumb: if you cannot say 'this is a strong version of X' or 'a mild version of X', the answer is intensity: 0. Always add a _flags note naming the reason so reviewers see the explicit decision.
senses._intensity_default_trap: Intensity 1 is NOT a safe default. It means 'genuinely first-stirring' (心動 / 有好感 / 有點). 5,599 senses sat at intensity=1 because prior framing made null feel like an empty field and 1 became the unconscious default. The 0-vs-null distinction makes Not Applicable an explicit positive choice (intensity: 0 with reasoning) versus an unfinished field (null). DO NOT skip Stage 1. Full specification: project_intensity_specification.md in memory.
senses._valency_note: 0 = intransitive (Vi, Vp, Vs, Vspred), 1 = transitive (V, Vpt, Vst, Vaux), 2 = ditransitive. null for non-verbs. Matches the guide exactly — every verb POS has a valency assignment.
senses._tocfl_CRITICAL: Do not override or invent TOCFL values; pass through what the source data provides. Skeleton batches carry TOCFL values pre-populated from official wordlists — keep them untouched. If a skeleton sense arrives with no TOCFL value, leave as null. Never derive a value from your own judgment of difficulty, frequency, or semantic similarity.
senses._hsk_CRITICAL: Do not override or invent HSK values; pass through what the source data provides. HSK levels come from official wordlists only — if the skeleton carries an HSK value, keep it; if not, leave as null. Never derive HSK from TOCFL or from semantic similarity.
senses._formula_note: REQUIRED: Both formula_en AND formula_zh must be provided. The word itself stays in Chinese in both versions. Slot labels in [] are translated to the target language. formula_en uses English slot labels, formula_zh uses Chinese slot labels. Both versions are stored in word_sense_notes per language. Single-field 'formula' is NOT accepted — always provide _en and _zh separately.

_formula_notation.brackets: [Verb], [Person], [Clause] -- variable slots the learner fills in. Labels should be grammatically or semantically specific.
_formula_notation.plus: + connects elements in sequence. Spaces around + for readability.
_formula_notation.slash: / separates alternative patterns. Each side is a complete pattern.
_formula_notation.no_brackets: Words without brackets are fixed constants in the pattern.

_formula_by_pos_en.Vsep: 聊天 / 跟 + [Person] + 聊天 -- MUST show both joined and split forms
_formula_by_pos_en.Vcomp: V + 住 (記住, 抓住, 擋住)
_formula_by_pos_en.fixed: 再見 / 對不起 / 不客氣 -- fixed expressions ARE the formula, no brackets needed

_formula_by_pos_zh.N: [所有者] + 姊姊 / [數字] + 封 + 信
_formula_by_pos_zh.Prn: 我 + [動詞/詞組] / [動詞] + 我
_formula_by_pos_zh.V: 穿 + [衣物/鞋子] / 告訴 + [人] + [事物/子句]
_formula_by_pos_zh.Vsep: 聊天 / 跟 + [人] + 聊天 -- 必須展示合併與分離兩種形式
_formula_by_pos_zh.Adv: 很 + [狀態詞] / 已經 + [動詞/詞組]
_formula_by_pos_zh.Vcomp: V + 住 (記住, 抓住, 擋住)
_formula_by_pos_zh.fixed: 再見 / 對不起 / 不客氣 -- 固定表達本身就是公式，不需要括號

_formula_rules: The word MUST appear in its own formula — in Chinese, in BOTH versions.
formula_en: slot labels in [] use English. formula_zh: slot labels in [] use Chinese.
The Chinese grammar and word order are identical in both versions — only the [] labels change.
Keep it short -- one or two patterns maximum.
Be specific about object types: [Food]/[食物] is better than [Object]/[物件] for 烤.
Do NOT include separable notes like (V-O: 走+路). The POS chip says Vsep.
Do NOT write definitions as formulas. 'to walk' is not a formula.
Formulas are for learners -- prioritize patterns needed to produce correct sentences.
senses.usage_note_en: Broader and more systemic than 漲. Common with prices, egos, ambitions, and physical objects.
senses.usage_note_zh: 比「漲」更廣泛、更系統性。常見於物價、自我膨脹、野心及物理膨脹。
senses._usage_note_note: REQUIRED: Both usage_note_en AND usage_note_zh must be provided. EN version for English-speaking learners, ZH version for Chinese-immersion learners. Both stored in word_sense_notes per language. Write each independently — do NOT translate word-for-word. Each should feel natural in its language. Single-field 'usage_note' is NOT accepted.
senses.learner_traps_en: Often confused with 擴大. 膨脹 implies internal pressure or uncontrolled growth; 擴大 implies deliberate expansion.
senses.learner_traps_zh: 容易和「擴大」搞混。膨脹強調內部壓力或不受控制的增長；擴大強調刻意的擴展。
senses._learner_traps_note: REQUIRED: Both learner_traps_en AND learner_traps_zh must be provided. These warn learners about common mistakes. A trap hidden in the language the learner is still learning is useless — it MUST be available in the learner's preferred language (LPL). Write each version naturally, not as a translation. Single-field 'learner_traps' is NOT accepted.
senses._relations_note: Semantic relations to other words. Use traditional characters. The importer will look up the word_object and sense.
senses._relations_DECISION_LADDER: Apply for EVERY relation, top to bottom: (1) Clear semantic opposite on a shared dimension? → antonym. (2) Same field but NOT substitutable? → contrast. (3) Nearly interchangeable in most contexts? → synonym_close. (4) Substitutable in some contexts with different nuance? → synonym_related. (5) None of the above? → DO NOT FORCE. Flag with _flags and leave the pair out.
senses._relations_CONTRAST_TEST: Before classifying a pair as `contrast`, write the shared dimension in ≤5 words. If you can't, it's NOT a contrast — move the distinction to learner_traps. Fails from past batches: 罷工↔抗議 (type-of, not polar), 癌症↔腫瘤 (is-a), 安慰↔鼓勵 (different target conditions), 扮演↔導演 (same domain, not contrasting).
senses._relations_CATEGORY_TRAP: Category relations are NOT synonymy: sequence neighbors (甲/乙/丙), hypernyms (菠菜 is-a 青菜), institution-vs-activity (補習/補習班), bound roots (軍 vs 部隊). None pass the substitution test. Keep the §9 flag; don't force category pairs into synonym_related.
senses._relations_FLAG_OVER_FAKE: 1 clean relation + _flags note > 2 relations where one is forced. Don't pad the relation layer to hit a count.

_relation_types.synonym_close: Very close synonym — nearly interchangeable in most contexts
_relation_types.synonym_related: Related synonym — similar meaning but different nuance, register, or scope
_relation_types.contrast: Not opposite, but commonly compared or confused with this word

senses._collocations_REQUIRED: Minimum 2 per sense. Must be natural phrases that learners would encounter, not formula fragments or thin shells. REJECT: the headword alone, headword+headword, bare [MW]+headword (一個愛好). ACCEPT: multi-word natural collocations (擔任班長, 保持聯絡, 保存完整). Use traditional characters.

examples.chinese: 通貨膨脹讓人民的生活越來越困難。
examples.english: Inflation has made people's lives increasingly difficult.

senses._examples_guidance: Provide at least 2 examples per sense. Each example should clearly demonstrate usage within one of the listed domains. Examples should feel natural. The word MUST appear in each example.

_global_rules: 1 sense = 1 POS = 1 definition block. If a word has Vi AND N meanings, create SEPARATE senses.
V IS a valid POS -- the default transitive action verb. Do not avoid it.
TOCFL is the authoritative source for POS classification. Editorial corrections must be tracked via source + alignment.
Use the forced 6-step test order to classify verbs. NEVER skip ahead.
Resultative complement present? -> Vpt/Vp. STOP. No further testing needed.
Vsattr is a SMALL CLOSED category -- only for words that CANNOT appear after 很 as predicates. Check TOCFL list.
Vsep is for V-O compounds (動賓) where V and O can be separated. V-V compounds with complements are Vpt/Vp, NOT Vsep.
Do NOT include separable metadata in definitions (e.g., 'separable: 走+路'). The POS chip communicates this.
Pronouns should use Prn (not N). Numbers should use Num (not N). Our correction over TOCFL's N classification.
sense = meaning; domains = lenses. The definition defines what it means; domains describe where it's used.
The first domain in the array is treated as primary.
Register, dimension, and domains are multi-select (arrays). Other attributes are single-select.
Channel describes spoken/written usage spectrum. Dimension describes semantic character. Do not confuse.
Pinyin uses numeric tones (liu2, not liú). Neutral tone = no number (de, ma, ba).
All _ prefixed fields are documentation and will be stripped on import.

BILINGUAL NOTES ARE REQUIRED: formula_en + formula_zh, usage_note_en + usage_note_zh, learner_traps_en + learner_traps_zh. Do NOT use single-field 'formula', 'usage_note', or 'learner_traps'. Each version feeds into word_sense_notes per language. Missing bilingual fields will be flagged in review.
Write EN and ZH notes INDEPENDENTLY — not as word-for-word translations. Each should feel natural in its language.

Huiming: Vsep avoidance -- tends to tag V-O compounds as Vp instead of Vsep
Vpt over-application -- some simple action verbs tagged as Vpt without resultative complement
N under-assigned for primary noun senses
Separable notes in definitions -- strip '(separable: X+Y)' from all definitions
Single-field notes -- uses 'formula'/'usage_note'/'learner_traps' instead of required _en/_zh bilingual fields
Antonym-as-contrast -- puts obvious antonym pairs into contrast bucket instead of antonym
Proximity-linking -- treats domain neighbors as synonyms (e.g. 動物 as synonym of 熊貓)
Guangliu: Vsattr over-applied to gradable Vs words
Vi treated as 'any intransitive' -- Vi = intransitive ACTION only, not process
Vst missed entirely -- apply 在-test before defaulting to V
Louie: Vst over-applied to active verbs -- 在-test is mandatory
Vi used as default intransitive -- intransitive process = Vp
N assigned to nominalization-only uses -- apply nominalization filter
